title: 1.4 - Response Translation & SSE Streaming
description:
Response Translation & SSE Streaming

## Scope
Translate upstream response chunks to client format. SSE transform stream with usage extraction.

## Subtasks
1. SSE event parser: parse lines, reconstruct JSON chunks, handle buffer boundaries
2. Response translator dispatch: target->source format conversion (reverse of request)
3. SINGLE [DONE] sentinel — 9router bug: DOUBLE [DONE] in translate AND passthrough modes
4. Token usage extraction from ALL formats with fallback estimation
5. Streaming handler: pipe provider Response through SSE transform to client
6. Non-streaming handler: SSE-to-JSON when provider forces streaming
7. Buffer size limit (prevent OOM from pathological provider chunks)
8. Cache-control-aware usage: buffer 2000 tokens to prevent context window errors

## CRITICAL 9router Fixes
- DOUBLE [DONE] sentinel: transform() emits one, flush() emits another. Fix: single emit with completion flag.
- toolArgBuffers Map not initialized: tool call arguments can vanish during streaming. Fix: pre-initialize.
- Error swallowing in flush(): all errors caught and logged but not propagated. Fix: controller.error() on failure.
- usage filter uses sourceFormat instead of post-translation format: non-OpenAI clients get empty usage. Fix: filter after translation.
- Gemini thoughtsTokenCount into prompt_tokens: misclassifies thinking as input. Fix: prepend to completion_tokens.

## Files
- src/stream/sse.rs — SSE line parser + serializer
- src/stream/transform.rs — Transform stream implementation
- src/stream/handler.rs — Streaming handler
- src/stream/json_handler.rs — SSE-to-JSON for non-streaming path

title: 2.3 - Account Fallback & Model-Level Locking
description:
Account Fallback & Model-Level Locking

## Scope
Model-level error handling with exponential backoff. Error classification: should_fallback vs non-recoverable. Rate limit cascade prevention.

## Subtasks
1. Model-level locking: modelLock_{model} timestamps per connection
2. Account-level lock fallback: modelLock___all when no specific model
3. Exponential backoff cooldown with CORRECT doubling (9router: off-by-one, level 0 and 1 both 2s)
4. Clear lock on successful request + lazy cleanup of expired locks
5. Error classification: should_fallback vs non-recoverable (400/404 should NOT fallback)
6. 429 TRANSIENT COOLDOWN before next combo model (9router: no delay = cascade)
7. Error text matching with ERROR_RULES config (substring-based, top-to-bottom priority)

## CRITICAL 9router Fixes
- shouldFallback ALWAYS true: default branch returns shouldFallback=true for ALL unmatched errors. Fix: 400/404/500 return false.
- Dead rateLimitedUntil field: active locking writes modelLock_*, not rateLimitedUntil. Fix: use only modelLock_* fields.
- Backoff off-by-one: getQuotaCooldown(max(0, level-1)). Fix: base * 2^level with no offset.
- 429 cascade with NO delay between combo models. Fix: apply cooldownMs before next model.
- filterAvailableAccounts checks rateLimitedUntil which is never written. Fix: use isModelLockActive.

## Files
- src/fallback/error.rs — ErrorRule matching, should_fallback logic
- src/fallback/backoff.rs — Exponential backoff calculation
- src/fallback/lock.rs — ModelLock read/write
- src/fallback/clear.rs — ClearAccountError + lazy cleanup

## Tests
- 429 triggers fallback with cooldown
- 400 (Bad Request) does NOT fallback
- Model lock prevents selection on next request
- Clear on success removes model lock
- Expired locks auto-cleaned
- Rate limit cascade prevented with delay
close_reason: Implemented and verified. Codebase has feature with tests passing.

title: 1.5 - Stream Controller & Connection Management
description:
Stream Controller & Connection Management

## Scope
Manage HTTP streaming connections with cancellation, stall detection, and cleanup.

## Subtasks
1. Stream controller with AbortController-based cancellation
2. Byte-level stall watchdog: measure upstream bytes, NOT transform output (9router fix: reasoning models pause SSE output)
3. Graceful terminal synthesis: Responses API response.failed + [DONE] on abort
4. Reader.cancel() + writer.abort() with await on cleanup paths (9router bug: unhandled promises)
5. Buffer size cap (prevent OOM from huge SSE lines)
6. Shared stateless encoder (TextEncoder)

## CRITICAL 9router Fixes
- Stall timeout race: timer callback runs handleError after stream already completed. Fix: guard timer with boolean flag.
- cancel() returns void without awaiting cleanup: unhandled promise rejections. Fix: await both paths.
- wrappedController bypass: stall timeout calls controller directly instead of wrapped methods. Fix: call wrapped methods.
- StreamHandler error.name without null guard: TypeError if non-Error thrown. Fix: optional chaining.

## Files
- src/stream/controller.rs — StreamController struct
- src/stream/stall.rs — Stall watchdog
- src/stream/disconnect.rs — Disconnect detection + cleanup

title: 2.4 - Combo & Fusion
description:
Combo & Fusion

## Scope
Multi-model strategies: sequential fallback, round-robin rotation, parallel fusion with judge synthesis.

## Subtasks
1. Combo definition: sequential model list from user config
2. FALLBACK strategy: try models in order, retry transient (502/503/504) with cooldown delay
3. ROUND-ROBIN strategy: rotate through models with sticky limit, track rotation state
4. FUSION strategy: parallel panel executors, quorum-grace collection, judge synthesis
5. Quorum-grace: min N panel responses -> short grace window for stragglers -> proceed
6. Fusion timeout: AbortController CANCELS in-flight requests on timeout (9router: leaks HTTP requests)
7. Lone-survivor fusion: use the ONE successful panel response directly (9router: re-requests model = double cost)
8. Capability auto-switch: detect vision/pdf/search requirements, reorder models to front
9. flattenToolHistory: convert tool turns to prose but assign role:user (9router: role:assistant creates consecutive assistants)

## CRITICAL 9router Fixes
- Fusion timeout LEAKS in-flight HTTP requests: no AbortController passed. Fix: pass cancel signal.
- Lone-survivor discards panel response, re-requests model. Fix: return existing response directly.
- flattenToolHistory creates consecutive assistant roles (tool->assistant). Fix: assign role:user or merge.
- Rotation state migration path incomplete: old {index} format causes NaN. Fix: check both formats.
- checkFallbackError in combo called WITHOUT backoffLevel. Fix: propagate backoff level.
- 429 cascade no delay. Fix: apply cooldownMs before next model.

## Files
- src/combo/strategy.rs — ComboStrategy enum + dispatch
- src/combo/fallback.rs — Sequential fallback
- src/combo/round_robin.rs — Round-robin with rotation state
- src/combo/fusion.rs — Parallel panel + judge synthesis
- src/combo/capability.rs — Required capabilities detection

## Tests
- Fallback tries models in order, stops on success
- Round-robin distributes evenly with sticky limit
- Fusion panel timeout cancels in-flight requests
- Lone-survivor returns existing result (no re-request)
- Capability auto-switch reorders vision models for image requests
- Rate limit delay prevents cascade
close_reason: Implemented and verified. Codebase has feature with tests passing.

title: 2.1 - Provider Credential Management
description:
Provider Credential Management

## Scope
Manage per-provider credentials with encrypted storage. Multi-account strategies and thread-safe selection.

## Subtasks
1. Credential storage: provider_id, auth_type (api_key, oauth, device_code), encrypted tokens, metadata
2. Fill-first strategy: sort by priority, pick first available
3. Round-robin with sticky limit: N consecutive requests to same account before rotating
4. Thread-safe account selection with tokio::sync::Mutex (not Promise-based: 9router mutex stuck bug)
5. Mutex MUST resolve in all paths: success, error, panic (9router: finally block missing)
6. Per-provider strategy overrides (fill-first per provider, round-robin per another)

## 9router CRITICAL Fixes
- Mutex stuck on exception: resolveMutex never called. Fix: use tokio::sync::Mutex RAII guard.
- Race between account selection and lock write: selected, then locked outside mutex. Fix: lock inside transaction.
- _connection live reference: concurrent updateProviderConnection causes stale reads. Fix: deep clone or read-latest.
- appending consecutiveUseCount outside mutex: clearAccountError/markAccountUnavailable bypass mutex. Fix: all writes through guarded methods.

## Files
- src/credentials/mod.rs — CredentialManager struct + strategies
- src/credentials/storage.rs — Encrypted storage (delegates to DB layer)
- src/credentials/selection.rs — Account selection algorithms

## Tests
- Fill-first picks highest priority available account
- Round-robin distributes evenly with sticky limit
- Mutex resolves in error path (inject panic in selection)
- Concurrent selection returns different accounts
close_reason: Implemented and verified. Codebase has feature with tests passing.

title: [BUG] OPENPROXY_ENCRYPTION_KEY mismatch causes encrypted keys to be sent to upstream
description:
## Bug
In `src/db/mod.rs`:
- **Write** (`update`, line 141): `encryption_key().unwrap_or_default()` → uses empty string `""` when env var unset
- **Read** (`read_app_db`, line 498): `if let Some(ref key) = encryption_key()` → SKIPS decryption when env var unset

Net effect: encrypted-with-empty-key blob is stored, then read back AS-IS (no decryption), and the encrypted blob is then sent as the upstream API key. Upstream returns "Invalid API key" / "Invalid token".

## Additional symptom
Each subsequent save RE-ENCRYPTS the already-encrypted blob, causing `apiKey` field to grow monotonically (472 → 920 → ...) on every restart.

## Fix
Set `OPENPROXY_ENCRYPTION_KEY` consistently when starting both the CLI (for setup) and the server (for runtime). Use the SAME value or encryption will be inconsistent.

## Files
- src/db/mod.rs (write uses `unwrap_or_default`)
- src/db/crypto.rs (read skips decryption if key is None)
- Suggested improvement: make write also use the key if env var is set (or fail loudly if both write and read should agree)
close_reason: crypto: opxenc1 prefix, empty key never encrypts, missing/wrong key clears ciphertext (never send blob upstream); tests green

title: 1.2 - Format Detection & Translation Pipeline
description:
Format Detection & Translation Pipeline

## Scope
Detect API format from endpoint path + request body shape. Dispatch translation through typed registry.

## Subtasks
1. Format enum: OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, GeminiCli, OpenAIResponses, Vertex, Codex, Antigravity, Kiro, Cursor, Ollama, CommandCode
2. fn detect_format(endpoint, body_headers, body_json) -> Format — rules-based detection
3. Translator registry dispatching by (source, target) tuple
4. Translation pipeline: detect -> resolve -> translate (direct or pivot through OpenAI)
5. Response symmetry enforcement: every request translator MUST have matching response translator
6. Source format override via endpoint path (/v1/messages -> Claude, /v1/responses -> Responses)

## Detection Rules (fixed from 9router)
- /v1/responses -> OpenAI Responses
- /v1/messages -> Claude
- body.anthropic_version -> Claude (check BEFORE messages heuristics)
- body.contents[] -> Gemini/Antigravity
- User-Agent is HTTP HEADER not body field (9router bug: reads body.userAgent)
- model string with / does NOT exclude Claude (9router bug: body.model.includes("/") blocks it)

## Files
- src/format.rs — Format enum + detection
- src/translator/mod.rs — TranslatorRegistry, dispatch
- src/translator/registry.rs — Registry builder

## Tests
- /v1/messages with Claude body -> Claude
- /v1/responses -> OpenAIResponses
- /v1/chat/completions with messages[] + anthropic_version -> Claude (NOT OpenAI)
- Gemini contents[] -> Gemini
- Unknown defaults -> OpenAI

## 9router CRITICAL Bugs Fixed
- Pure-text Claude without body.system misclassified as OpenAI
- body.model.includes("/") blocks Claude detection for antigravity/claude-sonnet
- body.userAgent read as body field instead of HTTP header

title: 1.3 - Request Translation (All Format Pairs)
description:
Request Translation (All Format Pairs)

## Scope
Implement 12 request translators for all supported format pairs. Each translator converts source body to target body.

## Translators
1. OpenAI -> Claude: role mapping (developer->system), tool_use/tool_result, images, thinking, cache_control, max_tokens preserved
2. Claude -> OpenAI: system message, tool_use->tool_calls, tool_result->role:tool, thinking STRIPPED (OpenAI doesn't support)
3. Gemini -> OpenAI: contents->messages, functionCall->tool_calls, thought/thinking blocks PRESERVED (9router bug: dropped)
4. OpenAI -> Gemini: messages->contents, system->system_instruction, tools->toolConfig, MULTIPLE tool results (9router bug: only first preserved)
5. OpenAI -> Kiro: proprietary format, max_tokens PRESERVED (9router bug: hardcoded 32000)
6. OpenAI -> Cursor: JSON format for protobuf, max_tokens PRESERVED (9router bug: replaced with minimum)
7. OpenAI -> Ollama: message format, tool_calls WITHOUT index field (9router bug: preserves index)
8. OpenAI -> CommandCode: tool result with tool_name mapping (9router bug: empty)
9-11: Antigravity, OpenAI Responses, misc
12: OpenAI->OpenAI (passthrough with normalization)

## CRITICAL 9router Fixes
- Developer role SILENTLY DROPPED -> map to system
- max_tokens HARDCODED 32000 (kiro) or replaced with minimum (cursor) -> preserve from request
- Tool call argument parse failure sends raw string -> fallback to {}
- Multiple functionResponse parts -> iterate ALL parts
- Thinking blocks dropped in gemini/claude translators -> preserve
- Multiple system messages overwritten -> concatenate all
- Tool result tool_name always empty -> build call_id-to-name mapping

## Files
- src/translator/request/openai_to_claude.rs
- src/translator/request/claude_to_openai.rs
- etc (one file per translator)

## Test Strategy
- Round-trip: translate(source->target), translate(target->source), verify no data loss
- Every content block type tested (text, image, tool_use, tool_result, thinking)
- max_tokens field MUST survive translation unchanged

title: 2.2 - Token Refresh & OAuth
description:
Token Refresh & OAuth

## Scope
Per-provider OAuth token refresh with retry, dedup, and edge case handling.

## Subtasks
1. Refresh dispatch table: provider -> refresh function
2. Jittered exponential backoff retry (not linear: 9router uses 1s/2s/3s with no jitter)
3. Refresh dedup: cache IN-FLIGHT Promise, not null result (9router: caches null for 10s, defeats retry)
4. Unrecoverable error detection: invalid_grant, refresh_token_reused -> STOP retry immediately
5. Vertex JWT minting with in-memory cache + TTL (thread-safe, atomic read+write)
6. getAllAccessTokens passes ALL connection fields including providerSpecificData (9router: drops it, breaks Kiro)

## 9router CRITICAL Fixes
- dedup caches null for 10s: executor.refreshCredentials returns null on network error, cached. Fix: only cache SUCCESS.
- Object.assign race on credentials: concurrent 401 requests race on same object. Fix: clone-then-swap.
- getAllAccessTokens drops providerSpecificData: Kiro needs clientId/secret/region. Fix: pass all fields.
- vertex unreachable via getAccessToken: guard checks refreshToken string type, vertex uses apiKey. Fix: per-provider guard.
- vertexTokenCache not thread-safe: concurrent minting overwrites each other. Fix: tokio::sync::RwLock.

## Files
- src/auth/refresh.rs — Refresh dispatcher + per-provider implementations

title: 1.1 - Model Parsing & Provider Resolution
description:
Model Parsing & Provider Resolution

## Scope
Parse model strings (provider/model format) into typed enum + model name. Entry point of every request.

## Subtasks

2. Implement parse_model using LAST slash split (fix 9router bug: firstSlash breaks multi-slash models)
3. Build provider alias map (kr->kiro, cl->claude, etc.)
4. Model alias resolution from DB (user-defined name -> provider/model)
5. Combo detection: no slash means check combo registry first
6. Reserved prefix protection: user-defined prefixes cannot shadow built-in IDs
7. Edge cases: /model (empty provider error), provider/ (empty model error), a/b/c (provider=a, model=b/c)

## Rust Files
- src/provider/id.rs — ProviderId enum + alias resolution
- src/model/parser.rs — parse_model + error types
- src/model/combo.rs — ComboDetector

## Error Types
ParseModelError: Empty, UnknownProvider, EmptyModel, EmptyProvider

## Tests
- "gpt-4o" -> (OpenAI, "gpt-4o")
- "kr/claude-sonnet" -> (Kiro, "claude-sonnet")
- "/model" -> Err EmptyProvider
- "provider/" -> Err EmptyModel
- "a/b/c" -> (a, b/c) using last slash
- Unknown prefix -> Err UnknownProvider

- Dead code combo re-detection (handleChat.js duplicated)
- Only firstSlash breaks multi-slash model names
- detectFormat misclassifies Claude as OpenAI enum prevents this
- Double parsing in wrapper + getModelInfoCore

title: 9router→OpenProxy Logic Parity Hardening (v0.5.30 audit)
description:
# Epic: 9router → OpenProxy Logic Parity Hardening

## Background
OpenProxy (Rust 0.1.2) ports 9router (JS 0.5.30, open-sse). Evidence audit against `/tmp/9router` found modules that exist but are unwired, wrong protocol surfaces, and pipeline-order mismatches. Small divergences break Claude Code, Kiro, Codex, fusion combos, DeepSeek TUI, GitHub Codex models.

Reference roots (read-only; do NOT copy JS bugs blindly):
- JS core: `/tmp/9router/open-sse/` — handlers, executors, translator, services, config
- JS entry: `/tmp/9router/src/sse/handlers/chat.js`
- Rust hot path: `src/server/api/chat.rs`
- Rust core: `src/core/{chat,combo,executor,translator,account_fallback,config}/`
- OAuth: `src/oauth/`

## Why this epic exists (project goals)
OpenProxy’s purpose (AGENTS.md): replace 9router with a faster, safer Rust router **without losing working client/provider behavior**. Type safety and encryption are wins only if drop-in routing still works. This epic is the bridge from “code exists” to “behavior matches.”

## Overarching goals
1. Behavioral parity on chat lifecycle for supported providers.
2. Keep intentional Rust improvements (document, never silently revert):
   - SSRF hardening on image fetch
   - Fail-loud MissingCredentials (vs Bearer undefined)
   - Combo quarantine + disabled members + capacity pre-skip (Rust extensions — document vs optional gate)
   - Refresh dedup does NOT cache null failures (JS bug)
   - Encrypted SQLite secrets, HMAC API keys
3. Prefer wiring existing dead Rust code over rewrites.
4. Every CRITICAL/HIGH item ships with unit + e2e/regression tests and structured decision logs.

## Phase tracks (dependency order)
| Phase | Focus |
|-------|--------|
| P0-A | Translator routing, direct pairs, response intermediate hop, registration |
| P0-B | Chat pipeline order, forceStream SSE→JSON, passthrough, DeepSeek-TUI, Accept |
| P0-C | Executors: Kiro, GitHub /responses, Cursor host, Codex, orphans, Default 429/noAuth |
| P0-D | OAuth: codebuddy-cn, github/copilot, merge, shouldRefresh, kiro external_idp/vertex |
| P1-combo | RR/autoswitch order, sticky, fusion grace+stream, final status, capability detect |
| P1-caps | targetFormat/transport/upstream/strip, detectFormat, thinkingUnified, modality |
| P2 | ERROR_RULES unify, comboStrategies schema, PXPIPE, docs, hedging wire-or-doc |
| Tests | Shared parity harness with decision logging |

## Inventory of audit findings embedded (do not drop)
CRITICAL: translator direct route missing; response hop uses raw chunk; antigravity wrong fn; response pairs unregistered; normalize_claude_passthrough orphan; forceStream+SSE→JSON missing; DeepSeek-TUI always non-stream; providerThinking after translate; Kiro URL/auth/EventStream; Cursor host; GitHub /responses; grok-cli missing; mimo-free/codebuddy-cn orphans; combo autoswitch before RR; fusion grace+judge stream; OAuth codebuddy-cn/github/copilot wrong.
HIGH: no resolveTransport/model targetFormat/upstream/stripList; anthropic-compatible→openai; detectFormat order; modality caps by format; passthrough still strips; prefetch incomplete; TTS filter; PXPIPE; thinkingUnified; sticky hardcode 1; combo status.max(500); Codex force/effort/prefetch; merge expires_at; CODEX_MAX_REFRESH_AGE; needs_refresh(None); Default 429 no URL fallback; noAuth missing; dual-auth anthropic-compat; header hooks; non-SSE stream guard; gemini-cli imageGen non-stream; clear all model locks on success.

## Definition of Done (epic)
- [ ] All P0 child epics closed with their leaf Success Criteria met
- [ ] Parity harness (`…mj1.8`) runs in CI (`cargo test parity_` or equivalent) with decision logs
- [ ] Golden-path e2e (mocked upstream): Claude Code passthrough, OpenAI→Claude, Kiro EventStream→SSE, Codex stream:false→JSON, combo RR+vision primary, GitHub codex→/responses, github+codebuddy-cn refresh wire-format tests
- [ ] `docs/parity-9router.md` lists intentional divergences + dispatch matrix
- [ ] `br dep cycles` empty; no orphan executor files outside mod.rs without ADR note
- [ ] AGENTS.md beads blurb updated to point at this epic (when closing)

## Test strategy (epic-level)
- Unit: pure decision functions (format detect, stream decision, fallback rules, rotation, merge)
- Snapshot: translator request/response pairs (existing insta snapshots + new direct-route cases)
- Integration: chat.rs path with mock HTTP (wiremock/httpmock) per provider
- E2E optional live: behind `OPENPROXY_LIVE_PARITY=1`, never required for CI green
- Logging: every request logs `route=direct|pivot`, `stream=`, `forceStreamSSE2JSON=`, `executor=`, `passthrough=`, `acc=`

## Out of scope
- openproxy-bug4 (encryption key), openproxy-bug5 (/v1/models custom) — independent
- Pure dashboard UI visual parity
- Copying known 9router bugs

## Parallelization guidance
Max parallel tracks after harness exists: P0-A, P0-B (except passthrough after hooks), P0-C (except codex after forceStream, github after oauth), P0-D, P1-combo (except caps after modality), P1-caps detectFormat.

title: P0-A Translator routing, direct pairs, response hop, registration
description:
# Phase P0-A: Translator Routing & Registration

## Background
9router translator tries a direct source:target pair first, then falls back to OpenAI pivot. OpenProxy always pivots through OpenAI and ignores registered direct pairs (claude→kiro, kiro→claude). Response step-2 also feeds the original provider chunk instead of the OpenAI intermediate. Several response pairs have implementations but are not registered. openai→antigravity is registered to the wrong transform function (gemini-cli instead of antigravity).

## Why This Phase First
Almost every non-OpenAI client↔non-OpenAI provider path is broken or lossy without correct routing. Fixing executors first would still produce wrong request/response shapes.

## Scope
- Direct-route lookup for request and response
- Response double-hop intermediate chaining
- Register missing response pairs
- Fix openai→antigravity registration
- Mount prepare_claude_request / filter_to_openai_format hooks
- Unit + snapshot tests

## Files
- src/core/translator/registry.rs
- src/core/translator/request/*
- src/core/translator/response/*
- src/core/translator/snapshots/

## Success Criteria
- claude→kiro uses direct request transform (not double hop)
- kiro→claude uses direct response transform
- openai→antigravity uses openai_to_antigravity_request
- Client Claude + provider Kiro stream produces Claude-shaped SSE
- All new paths covered by unit/snapshot tests with logging of chosen route

title: P0-B Chat pipeline order, forceStream, passthrough, DeepSeek-TUI
description:
# Phase P0-B: Chat Pipeline Order & ForceStream

## Background
9router handleChatCore has a carefully ordered pipeline. OpenProxy chat.rs reorders key steps and omits forceStream SSE→JSON, mis-handles DeepSeek-TUI streaming, injects providerThinking after translation, and never calls normalize_claude_passthrough on native Claude passthrough.

JS order (simplified):
format → bypass → transport/target → thinking → stream decision → passthrough? → modality if !passthrough → translate/normalizeClaude → tool dedupe → TTS filter → RTK → Headroom → Caveman → Ponytail → PXPIPE → execute → 401 refresh → forceStream SSE→JSON → stream/non-stream

Rust order (simplified):
auth/model → Accept mutates stream → bypass → plan → modality always → prefetch → translate → RTK → Headroom → thinking → Caveman → tool dedupe → dispatch (DeepSeek always non-stream) → 401 loop → proxy

## Scope
- Reorder thinking override before translate
- DeepSeek-TUI only force non-stream when body.stream !== true
- Wire forceStream registry + handleForcedSSEToJson path
- Wire normalize_claude_passthrough + mount claude_format module
- Skip modality strip/prefetch on passthrough
- Accept-header stream preference parity (do not override explicit stream:true)
- TTS tool filter; optional PXPIPE later (P2)

## Files
- src/server/api/chat.rs
- src/core/chat/mod.rs
- src/core/translator/request/claude_format.rs (+ mod.rs)
- src/core/media/responses/stream_to_json.rs
- provider registry / forceStream flags

## Success Criteria
- DeepSeek-TUI interactive (stream:true) receives SSE
- Claude Code passthrough runs normalize_claude_passthrough
- Codex/openai forceStream + client stream:false returns aggregated JSON
- providerThinking on/off/effort applied on source body before translate
acceptance_criteria: All 1.2.* closed; stream decision pure helper tested; forceStream SSE→JSON e2e mock; passthrough Claude normalize wired

title: Translator: direct-route request lookup (source:target first)
description:
# Implement direct-route lookup in translate_request

## Background
9router open-sse/translator/index.js (approx L77-103): if source≠target, look up requestRegistry.get(`${source}:${target}`). If found, run only that function. Otherwise pivot source→openai then openai→target.

OpenProxy src/core/translator/registry.rs translate_request always does OpenAI pivot and never looks up (source, target) even when registered (e.g. claude→kiro at registration L751+).

Without this, thinking/tool/image fidelity is lost on fragile pairs. 9router AGENTS.md explicitly warns OpenAI bridge is lossy and direct routes are preferred.

## Technical Approach
1. In TranslatorRegistry::translate_request, before pivot:
   - if source == target: normalization only (existing)
   - else if let Some(fn) = request_transforms.get(&(source, target)): run fn; then normalization; return
   - else: existing pivot
2. Log chosen path at debug: "direct source→target" vs "pivot via openai".
3. Keep apply_normalization_hooks after either path.

## Success Criteria
- Unit test: mock or real claude→kiro registration is invoked and openai→kiro is NOT when body is Claude-shaped.
- Existing OpenAI→X paths still work.
- Logging shows route selection.

## Test Plan
- Unit: registry tests for direct hit, pivot miss, same-format.
- Snapshot: claude→kiro request body fields (tool_use, thinking) preserved better than pivot (compare hashes or key fields).
- Do not regress openai→claude snapshots.

## Considerations
- Response path is separate bead.
- Do not remove pivot; many pairs only have openai bridges.
- credentials/stream args must pass through direct fn same as pivot.
design: TranslatorRegistry::translate_request: if source==target { normalize }; else if let Some(f)=request_transforms.get(&(source,target)) { f; normalize; return true}; else { source->OpenAI; OpenAI->target; normalize }. Never drop pivot path. Instrument with route metric.
acceptance_criteria: claude→kiro direct route (test double); pivot for unregistered; log route=direct|pivot
notes: [2026-07-12 03:35:22 UTC] Polish R3: design
close_reason: Implemented direct-route lookup in translate_request; pivot retained; debug logs route=direct|pivot

title: Translator: direct-route response + intermediate hop chaining
description:
# Implement direct-route + intermediate chaining in translate_response

## Background
JS response path (translator/index.js ~L159-195):
1. Try responseRegistry.get(`${targetFormat}:${sourceFormat}`) direct
2. Else: target→openai into results[], then for each intermediate r, openai→source

Rust translate_response (~L424-448):
1. source→OpenAI into results
2. For OpenAI→target, calls transform(chunk, state) on the ORIGINAL provider chunk, not intermediate results
3. No direct (source,target) lookup for pairs like kiro→claude

Parameter naming in chat.rs: translate_response(stream_target_format, stream_source_format, ...) matches JS provider-format-first then client-format.

## Technical Approach
1. Add direct lookup for (provider_fmt, client_fmt) when both ≠ OpenAI and pair registered.
2. Pivot: step1 provider→openai; step2 map over intermediate strings (or bytes) into client format.
3. Unit-test with a tiny synthetic transform chain.

## Success Criteria
- kiro→claude direct used when registered.
- Double-hop OpenAI intermediate is input to second transform (prove with test double that records inputs).
- Stream path in chat.rs still works with tool_name_map state.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests with fake transforms recording call order and inputs.
- Integration: mock kiro SSE bytes → Claude SSE events.
- Log path selection.

## Considerations
- State (ResponseTransformState) must remain persistent across chunks for both hops.
- Empty conversion from step1 should not invent garbage; match JS empty-array behavior.
design: translate_response(provider_fmt, client_fmt, chunk, state): (1) direct (provider,client) if registered; (2) else provider->OpenAI into intermediates Vec; (3) for each intermediate (or if empty passthrough lossy), OpenAI->client. Critical bug today: step3 uses raw chunk. Fix to use intermediates. State persists across SSE chunks.
acceptance_criteria: Direct response pairs used; intermediate OpenAI chunks feed step2 (test double); kiro→claude stream fixture green
notes: [2026-07-12 03:35:22 UTC] Polish R3: design
close_reason: translate_response feeds intermediate OpenAI chunks to step2; direct route supported

title: Chat: DeepSeek-TUI only force non-stream when stream!==true
description:
# Fix DeepSeek-TUI stream decision to match 9router

## Background
JS chatCore.js ~L93-97:
  if (detectedTool === "deepseek-tui" && body.stream !== true) stream = false;
Comment: interactive TUI needs SSE; non-interactive (-p) cannot parse SSE.

Rust chat.rs ~L898-904:
  if client_tool == Some(DeepseekTui) { stream = false } always.

This breaks interactive DeepSeek TUI sessions that send stream:true.

## Technical Approach
Change condition to only force false when stream is not explicitly true (missing or false). Match JS strict equality on true.

Also review Accept-header mutation: Rust forces body.stream=false even when client sent stream:true if Accept prefers JSON — JS only adjusts when body.stream !== true and !providerRequiresStreaming. Align Accept logic in same bead or sibling.

## Success Criteria
- stream:true + DeepSeek-TUI UA → upstream stream true / SSE response path.
- stream absent + DeepSeek-TUI → non-stream.
- stream:false + DeepSeek-TUI → non-stream.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests on stream decision helper (extract if needed).
- E2E with mocked UA header deepseek-tui.

## Considerations
- Extract stream decision into a pure function for testability (mirrors chatCore logic block).
acceptance_criteria: DeepSeek-TUI stream:true keeps SSE; absent/false non-stream; unit table + UA fixture
notes: [2026-07-12 03:35:51 UTC] Polish R4: MUST share resolve_stream_flags with 1.2.3, 1.2.5, 1.2.8 — extract once in first of these that lands; others call helper.

title: Chat: forceStream registry + SSE→JSON for non-stream clients
description:
# Implement forceStream + SSE-to-JSON aggregation path

## Background
JS: PROVIDERS[provider].forceStream === true forces stream=true upstream; if !clientRequestedStreaming && providerRequiresStreaming, handleForcedSSEToJson aggregates SSE into JSON (sseToJsonHandler.js). Providers with forceStream in 9router registry include codex, openai (some transports), commandcode, codebuddy-cn, grok-cli.

Rust: no chat-level forceStream; stream_to_json.rs exists but unused from chat.rs. Clients requesting stream:false against force-stream providers get wrong content-type/body.

clientRequestedStreaming in JS also treats antigravity/gemini/gemini-cli formats as streaming clients.

## Technical Approach
1. Add force_stream flag to provider config / registry used by chat plan.
2. Compute client_requested_streaming and provider_requires_streaming like JS.
3. When provider forces stream but client wants JSON: execute stream=true upstream, collect SSE, convert via sse_stream_to_json / convert_responses_stream_to_json, return application/json.
4. Codecs: support both chat-completions SSE and responses SSE (codex).

## Success Criteria
- Codex + stream:false returns single JSON object (not SSE).
- stream:true still streams.
- Force-stream providers listed match 9router registry intent.

## Test Plan
- Unit: sse_stream_to_json fixtures already exist — wire integration test from chat layer with mock upstream SSE.
- E2E: codex-like SSE → JSON collapse.
- Logging: "forceStream SSE→JSON" line.

## Considerations
- codebuddy_cn executor also forces stream — coordinate with P0C wire bead.
- Abort/disconnect during aggregation should map to 499/cancel like JS where possible.
design: Extract resolve_stream_flags(body, headers, provider, model_type, client_tool) -> StreamPlan { stream, client_requested, provider_forced, sse_to_json }. forceStream from provider registry. On sse_to_json: execute stream=true, collect with stall timeout, sse_stream_to_json / convert_responses_stream_to_json, return JSON response. Logging: FORCE_STREAM provider=.. client_requested=.. sse_to_json=..
acceptance_criteria: forceStream+client stream:false → application/json; stream:true SSE; unit+mock DecisionTrace; non-force providers unchanged
notes: [2026-07-12 03:35:22 UTC] Polish R3: design notes for stream plan helper
close_reason: forceStream providers + sse_to_json via proxy_sse_to_json_response; stream_to_json module wired

title: Chat: Accept-header must not override explicit stream:true
description:
# Chat: Accept-header must not override explicit stream:true

## Background
JS chatCore.js ~L99-106: if Accept has application/json, lacks text/event-stream, body.stream !== true, and !providerRequiresStreaming → local stream=false (does not mutate body when stream explicitly true).
Rust chat.rs ~L330-341: wants_json && !wants_sse && accept != "*/*" → inserts body.stream=false even if client sent stream:true. Extra `*/*` exclusion is Rust-only.

## User impact / failure mode
AI SDK or curl clients that send both Accept: application/json and stream:true (common) incorrectly get non-streaming JSON, breaking progressive UIs.

## Technical Approach
1. Extract pure `apply_accept_stream_preference(accept, stream_field, provider_requires_streaming) -> bool`.
2. Only force false when stream is not explicitly true AND !provider_requires_streaming.
3. Prefer not mutating body; pass resolved stream flag downstream (or mutate only when field absent).
4. Integrate with forceStream bead (1.2.3): providerRequiresStreaming blocks Accept force-down.

## Success Criteria
| Accept | body.stream | forceStream | result stream |
|--------|-------------|-------------|---------------|
| application/json | true | false | true |
| application/json | absent | false | false |
| application/json | false | false | false |
| text/event-stream | absent | false | true (default) |
| application/json | false | true | true (upstream) + SSE→JSON if client non-stream |

## Test Plan
- Unit table-driven (all rows above + `*/*` + multi-value Accept).
- Integration: POST with Accept json + stream true → mock sees stream true.
- Log: `ACCEPT_STREAM prefer_json=.. resolved_stream=..`.

## Considerations
- Coordinate with DeepSeek-TUI and imageGen stream helpers — single resolve_stream_flags module preferred.
- Do not break pure JSON AI SDK clients (Accept json, no stream field).
notes: [2026-07-12 03:34:43 UTC] Polish R1: deepened description, failure modes, detailed test plan.
close_reason: Accept preference in resolve_stream_flags does not override stream:true

title: Executor: Kiro URL/auth/EventStream→SSE 9router parity
description:
# Kiro executor: match 9router protocol (URL, auth, EventStream→SSE)

## Background
9router registry kiro.js:
- baseUrl: https://runtime.us-east-1.kiro.dev/generateAssistantResponse
- baseUrls also include codewhisperer and q amazonaws generateAssistantResponse
- Auth: Bearer accessToken; headers tokentype API_KEY / TokenType EXTERNAL_IDP as appropriate
- Response: AWS EventStream decoded to SSE inside executor

Rust kiro.rs:
- Path /v1/{model}/{stream|invoke} on runtime.*.kiro.dev only
- Auth: SigV4 if access_token is JSON AWS creds; else x-api-key
- EventStreamDecoder implemented and unit-tested but NOT called in execute_request — raw binary returned to client

This is total incompatibility for real Kiro users.

## Technical Approach
1. Align URL construction with 9router baseUrls + generateAssistantResponse.
2. Auth-aware URL ordering (api_key/external_idp/idc → amazonaws first) per kiro.js getOrderedBaseUrls.
3. Headers: Bearer, TokenType, Amz-Sdk-* as JS.
4. On success, transform EventStream body/stream through EventStreamDecoder into SSE/text before returning UpstreamResponse (or wrap stream).
5. Keep regional failover spirit; do not invent new surfaces.

## Success Criteria
- Fixture EventStream bytes → SSE events with expected types.
- Request URL path contains generateAssistantResponse (or documented intentional new API with feature flag — default must match 9router).
- Auth header scheme matches credential type (social/API key/IDC).

## Test Plan
- Unit: EventStreamDecoder end-to-end already partial — extend with recorded frames from 9router tests if available.
- Unit: URL ordering by credential type.
- Integration: mock HTTP returning eventstream content-type → client sees text/event-stream.

## Considerations
- Do not break any in-progress AWS SigV4 path if Kiro still uses it for some account types — map carefully from 9router branches.
- Token refresh kiro external_idp is P0D related.
design: Align with open-sse/executors/kiro.js + registry/kiro.js. URL: generateAssistantResponse on kiro.dev + codewhisperer + q hosts. Auth branches: Bearer+TokenType, API_KEY, IDC. On response: EventStreamDecoder::decode_chunk or stream transform to text/event-stream before return. Do not leave decoder as dead code.
acceptance_criteria: generateAssistantResponse hosts; EventStream→SSE; auth by credential type; unit+mock HTTP
notes: [2026-07-12 03:35:22 UTC] Polish R3: design
close_reason: Kiro URLs generateAssistantResponse; Bearer+tokentype auth; ordered amazonaws for api_key

## Background

| Provider | Action |
|----------|--------|
| mimo-free | Wire mod+dispatch OR delete file and fix Default URL + document unsupported bootstrap |
| codebuddy-cn | Wire specialized (force stream + correct URL) OR Default with force stream |
| ollama-local | Wire ollama.rs to chat for provider ollama-local / ollama |
| grok-cli | Implement specialized Responses executor (not xai api.x.ai) or explicit unsupported error |

Aliases: gcli/gb→grok-cli, mmf→mimo-free, cu already handled.

## Success Criteria
- No orphan .rs outside mod.rs without `// intentionally unmaintained` + issue link.

## Test Plan

- Unit URL builders.
- Dispatch test: provider id → executor kind enum/log.
- Mock smoke per wired provider.

## Considerations

# DefaultExecutor: 429 multi-URL fallback + noAuth flag

## Background
JS BaseExecutor.shouldRetry: status 429 with remaining baseUrls → next URL. config.noAuth skips 401 refresh in chatCore.

Multi-region OpenAI-compatible setups never rotate on 429; free/local providers attempt useless refresh on 401.

2. On 429, advance URL index like JS (respect max retries).
3. noAuth on provider registry (ollama-local, public demos) → skip refresh in chat and DefaultExecutor.
4. Unit-test retry sequence: 429,429,200 across URLs.

## Success Criteria
- Three baseUrls, first two 429, third 200 → success.
- noAuth provider 401 → no refresh call (mock counter).

## Test Plan

- Log: `RETRY status=429 next_url_index=i`.

# OAuth: codebuddy-cn refresh header/JSON protocol parity

## Background

3. Preserve refresh token if not rotated; merge via 1.4.3 helper.
4. Cover codebuddy vs codebuddy-cn dispatch arms.

## Success Criteria
- Mock server validates X-Refresh-Token + empty JSON body.
- code=0 → access token updated.
- code!=0 → Err, no silent success.

- Golden request headers snapshot (token redacted).
- Log: `REFRESH provider=codebuddy-cn ok|err`.

## Considerations

Encrypted fields must go through existing DB crypto paths.

Used by chat 401, DefaultExecutor, CredentialManager.

4. Use refresh_lead(provider) in proactive checks.

## Success Criteria
- Codex: last_refresh_at >8d ago → proactive refresh even if access token not near expiry.
- Missing expires_at → no proactive refresh.

title: Combo: apply capability autoswitch after RR rotation
description:
# Combo: apply capability autoswitch AFTER RR rotation

## Background
JS combo.js ~L229-242: `rotatedModels = getRotatedModels(...); if (autoSwitch) reorderByCapabilities(rotatedModels, required)`.
Rust chat.rs ~L384-393 then execute rotates: **reorder first, then rotate**.

Counterexample: members [A,B,C], RR index=1, only C has vision.
- JS: rotate→[B,C,A] → autoswitch→[C,B,A]
- Rust: autoswitch→[C,A,B] → rotate→[A,B,C]

## User impact / failure mode
Vision requests hit non-vision primary under round-robin → 400 or useless text-only answers; different model bills than 9router.

## Technical Approach
1. Move reorder_by_capabilities to after get_rotated_models inside execute_combo_strategy* (not in chat before call).
2. Keep autoSwitch default on when caps non-empty (JS default true).
3. Port unit cases from `/tmp/9router/tests/unit/combo-autoswitch.test.js` and combo-routing.test.js.

## Success Criteria
Fixture above yields C-first under RR index=1 + vision body.

## Test Plan
- Unit pure functions: rotate then reorder with fixed index.
- Property: for sticky=1, vision request primary has vision cap when any member has it.
- Log: `COMBO_ORDER before=.. after_rr=.. after_caps=..`.

## Considerations
- Depends on accurate capability detect (1.5.5) for real models; this bead fixes ordering even with heuristic caps.
acceptance_criteria: RR then autoswitch order matches fixture C-first; unit from 9router tests; DecisionTrace logs order

title: Combo fusion: grace timer + judge streaming parity
description:
# Combo fusion: independent straggler grace timer + judge streaming

## Background
JS fusion: when ok panels >= minPanel, setTimeout(finish, stragglerGraceMs) independent of other panel completions; judge uses original body stream flag (preserves stream:true).
Rust fusion.rs: tightens hard_deadline but only rechecks when another future completes — no independent grace sleep; judge forces stream:false; chat returns plain JSON string body for fusion success.

Defaults (both): minPanel=2, grace=8000ms, hard=90000ms.

## User impact
- Fusion latency = slowest panel hard timeout (90s) instead of ~8s after quorum.
- Streaming clients get a single JSON blob instead of SSE → UI freezes until end.

## Technical Approach
1. tokio::select! { biased; grace_sleep => finish, panel = next => handle }
2. Preserve client stream for judge body; if stream, chat path must stream judge SSE (may need execute_single_model stream=true + proxy stream).
3. Keep panels non-stream + tools stripped + tool-history flatten.
4. Fix panel text extraction to accept array content parts (not only string content) — JS extractTextContent.
5. Config: FusionConfig from combo.extra; later nested settings (1.7.2).

## Success Criteria
- 2/3 panels done → return within grace+epsilon, not full hard timeout (unit with delayed third).
- Client stream:true → response content-type text/event-stream (or documented streaming JSON lines).
- Single panel → direct dispatch; zero ok → 503.

## Test Plan
- Unit grace timer with mock delayed futures.
- Unit text extraction array content.
- Integration fusion stream content-type.
- Port ideas from `/tmp/9router/tests/unit/combo-fusion.test.js`.
- Log: `FUSION quorum=.. grace_ms=.. judge_stream=..`.

## Considerations
- Blocks 1.7.2 schema only softly (already dep).
- Do not lose anonymized Source N judge prompt.
design: Fusion: panels force non-stream; on each completion if ok>=min_panel start/reset grace Deadline via tokio::select! { panel, sleep(grace) }. Judge: preserve client stream flag; if stream, return SSE body from chat (not only JSON Value). Config: FusionConfig from combo.extra + future nested settings.
acceptance_criteria: Grace timer independent; judge streaming for stream:true; delayed panel unit; fusion tests ported

# Combo: final error status uses first failure (not max 500)

## Background

Clients treat 500 as server bug and don't back off; rate-limit 429 lost.

## Technical Approach
1. Track first_status and first_message in combo loop.
2. Remove max(500) clamp (or only apply when status==0).
3. no-credentials across members → 503 with clear message.
4. Preserve earliest_retry_after aggregation.

## Success Criteria
- All members return 429 → combo response 429 (or documented 503 with Retry-After — default match JS first status).
- Mix 401 then 503 → 401 if first was 401.

## Test Plan

- Assert status and body error code.

## Considerations

title: Chat: non-SSE content-type guard on streaming path
description:
# Stream path: reject non-SSE upstream content-types (9router streamingHandler)

## Background
JS `streamingHandler.js` (~L62-79): when client expects SSE, if upstream Content-Type is not event-stream (e.g. HTML error page, application/json error), return a structured JSON error instead of piping garbage bytes as SSE. OpenProxy `proxy_response_with_pending_tracking` streams bytes without this preflight. Users see broken SSE parsers and hung clients.

## User impact
Any provider returning 200 with wrong content-type (CDN login page, JSON error with 200) corrupts client tool streams (Claude Code, Codex CLI).

## Technical Approach
1. After upstream response headers arrive, if plan.stream && client expects SSE:
   - If content-type is application/json or text/html (not event-stream), buffer body, map to createErrorResult-equivalent ComboAttemptError or SSE error event once.
2. Match JS message style where practical.
3. Log `STREAM_GUARD content-type=... status=...`.

## Success Criteria
- Mock upstream 200 text/html → client gets error JSON or single SSE error, not HTML chunks as data.
- Real event-stream still pipes through.

## Test Plan
- Unit/integration: mock response content-types (event-stream, json, html, missing).
- Detailed log assertion on guard trip.
- E2E: chat completions stream=true with mock HTML body.

## Considerations
- Some providers omit content-type; fail-open only if body looks like SSE (`data:` lines) else error after first chunk heuristic — document choice; prefer JS behavior if known.
- Dashboard stream re-encoding path must still work.
acceptance_criteria: Non-SSE upstream content-type does not pipe as SSE; mock html/json cases; event-stream still pipes

title: DefaultExecutor: anthropic-compatible dual-auth + token precedence
description:
# DefaultExecutor: anthropic-compatible dual-auth + identity header strip

## Background
JS default.js (~L171-201): for anthropic-compatible third-party gateways, dual Authorization strategies and strip identity headers / beta headers that break proxies. OpenProxy DefaultExecutor missing dual-auth and identity strip — common failure mode for third-party Claude-compatible endpoints.

Also: JS prefers accessToken over apiKey for Bearer; Rust `api_key.or(access_token)` inverts precedence — include in this bead.

## User impact
Custom anthropic-compatible provider nodes fail auth or get rejected by upstream due to wrong header set.

## Technical Approach
1. Port dual-auth + header strip from JS default.js for anthropic-compatible-* and provider_node type anthropic-compatible.
2. Align token precedence: access_token preferred over api_key for Bearer (match JS) unless node config says otherwise.
3. Log which auth mode was chosen (never log secrets).

## Success Criteria
- anthropic-compatible node with only access_token works.
- Identity headers that JS strips are stripped.
- Unit tests for header map construction.

## Test Plan
- Unit: build_headers matrix (api_key only, access_token only, both, anthropic-compatible).
- Integration: mock upstream asserts Authorization / x-api-key presence.

## Considerations
- Do not break native Anthropic x-api-key path.
- Coordinate with noAuth bead (1.3.6).

title: DefaultExecutor: Kimi/Cline/Claude header-cache hooks

# DefaultExecutor: header hooks (Kimi, Cline, Claude header cache overlay)

## Background
JS default.js uses HEADER_HOOKS: buildKimiHeaders, buildClineHeaders, claudeOverlay from claudeHeaderCache (session headers replayed for Claude ecosystem). OpenProxy caches Claude headers (`claude_header_cache`) but DefaultExecutor does not apply overlay; Kimi/Cline hooks missing → session continuity and provider-specific headers break.

## User impact
- Claude multi-turn session headers lost across requests
- Kimi/Cline provider-specific required headers absent → 4xx

## Technical Approach
1. Wire claude header cache overlay into DefaultExecutor (and specialized Claude paths if needed) matching JS claudeOverlay.
2. Port Kimi and Cline header builders from JS.
3. Config-driven hooks table so new providers can register without if-else soup.

## Success Criteria
- After a Claude request caches headers, subsequent request includes overlay keys JS would send.
- Kimi/Cline fixtures match JS header keys (values redacted in logs).

## Test Plan
- Unit: cache then overlay.
- Unit: kimi/cline header presence from credentials.provider_specific_data.

## Considerations
- Never log full Authorization.
- Cache TTL/security: do not leak headers across different API keys/users — scope cache like JS.

title: Account: selective model-lock clear on success (not clear-all)
description:
# Model lock clear on success: selective (JS) vs clear-all (Rust)

## Background
JS clearAccountError: clears the succeeded model lock + expired locks selectively.
Rust clear_connection_error: wipes ALL modelLock_* keys on any success.
Impact: a successful request for model A re-enables model B that was 404-locked, causing flapping retries and wasted quota.

## User impact
After one success, previously 404 models are retried immediately → error storms / slower fallback.

## Technical Approach
1. Port selective clear: clear lock for the model that succeeded + purge expired keys only.
2. Keep option to clear all on explicit admin reset.
3. Align mark_connection_unavailable 404 model-specific lock (already partially present) with clear path.

## Success Criteria
- Success on model A leaves active lock on model B intact until expiry.
- Expired locks cleaned.

## Test Plan
- Unit: two model locks, success on one, assert other remains.
- Integration: 404 B, success A, next request still skips B.

## Considerations
- Interaction with combo quarantine map (separate) — document.
close_reason: Implemented against 9router v0.5.30 open-sse; parity-smoke green; see docs/parity-9router.md

title: Executor: implement grok-cli (cli-chat-proxy Grok Build)
description:
# Executor: implement grok-cli (Grok Build / cli-chat-proxy)

## Background
9router has a specialized executor `open-sse/executors/grok-cli.js` + registry `providers/registry/grok-cli.js` for **Grok CLI / Grok Build** on `https://cli-chat-proxy.grok.com/v1/responses`.

This is **not** the same product as:
- `xai` → `api.x.ai` (API key / Grok Build OAuth PKCE, already partially ported as `xai.rs`)
- `grok-web` → grok.com web SSO cookie (`grok_web.rs`)

Deep scan after closing the 9router-parity epic found `grok-cli` completely missing from OpenProxy:
- No `src/core/executor/grok_cli.rs`
- Not in `executor/mod.rs`
- Not in `chat.rs` dispatch
- Aliases `gcli`, `grok-build`, `gb` unhandled

## JS surface (must match)
- **URL**: `https://cli-chat-proxy.grok.com/v1/responses` (Responses API)
- **forceStream**: true (registry) → chat forceStream + SSE→JSON path already exists
- **Auth**: OAuth device-code; Bearer access token; headers:
  - `User-Agent: grok-pager/0.2.93 grok-shell/0.2.93 (linux; x86_64)`
  - `x-xai-token-auth: xai-grok-cli`
  - `x-grok-client-identifier: grok-pager`
  - `x-grok-client-version: 0.2.93`
  - `x-authenticateresponse: authenticate-response`
  - plus turn/session headers (`x-grok-turn-idx`, session/machine ids) from executor
- **OAuth**: client_id `b1a00492-073a-47ea-816f-4c329264a828`, device/token URLs on `auth.x.ai`, scope includes `grok-cli:access`
- **Body**: Responses allowlist; strip server item refs when `store=false`; normalize tools (flatten function + keep hosted web_search/x_search); effort suffix `-high|-medium|-low` → reasoning effort; upstream model id via catalog
- **Refresh**: via `refreshProviderCredentials("grok-cli", …)` / same token URL as xai with grok-cli scope

## Technical Approach
1. Port provider registry entry (models, forceStream, oauth metadata, aliases).
2. New `GrokCliExecutor` (specialized, not Default): build URL, headers, transform request (Responses shape, effort strip, tool normalize, strip stored refs).
3. Wire into `executor/mod.rs` + `chat.rs` dispatch for `grok-cli` / `gcli` / `grok-build` / `gb`.
4. OAuth login + `dispatch_oauth_refresh` arm for `grok-cli` (may share xai token endpoint with correct client_id/scope).
5. Ensure forceStream list includes `grok-cli`.
6. Unit tests: URL, headers (no secrets logged), effort strip, tool flatten, turn-idx monotonicity.

## Success Criteria
- `provider=grok-cli` (and aliases) hits specialized executor, not Default/xai/grok-web.
- Upstream URL is cli-chat-proxy `/v1/responses`.
- stream:false client still works via existing SSE→JSON path.
- OAuth refresh updates access token for grok-cli connections.
- Models `grok-4.5(-high|-medium|-low)` resolve effort + upstream id correctly.

## Test Plan
- Unit: header builder, effort resolution, tool normalize, strip server ids.
- Mock HTTP: assert path `/v1/responses` and required headers.
- Integration: forceStream SSE→JSON for stream:false.
- Log: `EXECUTOR provider=grok-cli mode=responses force_stream=true`.

## Considerations
- Do not conflate with `xai` or `grok-web` — separate product surfaces.
- Reuse Codex/Responses helpers where shapes overlap; keep Grok-specific headers and turn store.
- Reference: `/tmp/9router/open-sse/executors/grok-cli.js`, `providers/registry/grok-cli.js`.
close_reason: New GrokCliExecutor

# Executor: complete xiaomi-tokenplan dual-transport (Claude path)

## Background
Partial port already exists in OpenProxy DefaultExecutor:
- Region sgp/cn/ams base URL resolution
- Always `{base}/chat/completions` + Bearer

9router `XiaomiTokenplanExecutor` also switches on `credentials.runtimeTransport.format === "claude"`:
- Claude → `{base without /v1}/anthropic/v1/messages` + `x-api-key` + Claude headers
- OpenAI → `{base}/chat/completions` + Bearer

Also: `resolve_transport` static table does **not** include `xiaomi-tokenplan` (or `xiaomi-mimo`), so Claude clients never get native transport and always pivot/translate.

Distinct from `mimo-free` (free JWT bootstrap).

## Technical Approach
1. Add `xiaomi-tokenplan` (+ alias `xmtp` if used) to `provider_transports` / resolve_transport with openai + claude endpoints per region (region from PSD; default sgp).
2. In DefaultExecutor `build_url` for xiaomi-tokenplan: if runtime_transport format is Claude (or path indicates anthropic), use `/anthropic/v1/messages` and strip trailing `/v1`.
3. Auth: Claude transport → `x-api-key` (raw key); OpenAI → Bearer.
4. Wire alias `xmtp` in chat/default if missing.
5. Unit: region × format URL matrix; header scheme matrix.

## Success Criteria
- sgp+claude → `https://token-plan-sgp.xiaomimimo.com/anthropic/v1/messages`
- cn+openai → `https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1/chat/completions`
- Claude client to xiaomi-tokenplan uses native Claude format (passthrough or targetFormat claude) without OpenAI pivot when transport matches.

## Test Plan
Unit build_url + resolve_transport; log `XIAOMI_TOKENPLAN region= format= url=`.

## Considerations
API keys are cluster-specific; wrong region fails auth. Keep known-host allowlist.
